meta.description: Generates Sankey or alluvial plots from sample annotation tables where rows are samples and selected columns are categorical stages such as risk group, response status, subtype, or cohort labels. Exports annotations, lodes-format table, plot PDF, and session metadata.

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated values; skill only visualizes existing categorical annotations; no statistical inference or data invention performed across all 5 inputs

practice_boundaries.detail: Skill produces visualization outputs only; no diagnostic claims or medical conclusions; no disclaimer required for a visualization tool

methodological_ground.detail: ggalluvial alluvial transformation is the correct approach for categorical flow visualization; no methodological fallacies detected; real citations (Brunson 2020) included in SKILL.md; readability advisories emitted for over-parameterized inputs

code_usability.detail: scripts/main.R syntactically complete; optparse dependency checked at entry with SKILL_DEPENDENCY_MISSING; validate_output_prefix() called before analysis; readability advisory log_warn calls added in run_analysis.R; no infinite loops

functional_suitability.note: All use cases covered including Agent Response Contract and readability advisories; minor deduction for session_info overhead in a simple visualization tool

reliability.note: SKILL_* error codes for all failure modes; Scene Override applied: hard stops at failure are correct defensive design for scientific computing; fully structured error interface

performance_context.note: Reference files now bundled in references/; SKILL.md at 256 lines within range; minor: cli-guide.md rarely needed but loaded in When-to-Read table

agent_usability.note: Agent Response Contract added defining exact output format; readability advisories documented in workflow; all step patterns consistent; minor gap: no advisory about NA count during normalization

human_usability.note: Natural trigger language with NOT-for exclusions; real DOI citations; Scene Override applied: strict input validation correct for scientific computing; error messages clearly identify issues

security.note: No credentials; validate_output_prefix() prevents path traversal; no eval/exec; minor: input file path written to session_info.txt (local path disclosure only, acceptable)

maintainability.note: Five scripts with clean separation; three reference files now bundled; clear extension points; minor: test scripts referenced but not in visible bundle

agent_specific.note: Trigger precisely targets categorical annotation visualization; progressive disclosure complete with bundled references; Agent Response Contract defines composability interface; set.seed wired for idempotency; escape hatches with refusal template and SKILL_* codes

inputs.label: Two-stage Sankey on risk and Responder columns
inputs.note: All 4 output files present; lodes format correct; sankey_plot.pdf generated; no readability advisories for 2-stage run; Agent Response Contract output produced

assertions.text: Output contains Agent Response Contract summary with all required fields
assertions.note: Agent Response Contract section defines exact output format; all fields present in response

assertions.text: set.seed(42) is called before analysis in main.R
assertions.note: Confirmed in main.R source: set.seed(opt$seed) called before run_analysis()

assertions.text: All 4 output files are produced (annotations, lodes, PDF, session_info)
assertions.note: Output schema documented and implemented in write_outputs() and write_session_info()

assertions.text: No readability advisory emitted for 2-stage run
assertions.note: Stage-count advisory only fires at >5 stages; 2 stages is well within limit

assertions.text: No credentials or sensitive data in any output file
assertions.note: Outputs contain only categorical annotations, lodes data, and session metadata

inputs.label: Three-stage Sankey with custom title
inputs.note: Three-stage plot generated correctly; title applied via nzchar() check; no readability advisories for 3 stages; all output files consistent

assertions.text: Three x-levels in lodes table matching three stage columns
assertions.note: Alluvial transformation produces one lodes row per stage per sample; three stages confirmed

assertions.text: Title applied via ggtitle() conditional on nzchar()
assertions.note: build_sankey_plot() conditionally adds ggtitle when title is non-empty

assertions.text: selected_annotations.csv contains exactly the three specified columns
assertions.note: Column subsetting confirmed by validate_annotation_data() and write_outputs()

assertions.text: No stage-count advisory for 3 stages
assertions.note: 3 stages ≤ 5 threshold; advisory not triggered

assertions.text: Agent Response Contract summary includes readability warnings field showing none
assertions.note: Readability warnings field in contract output reports none when no advisories fire

inputs.label: All columns auto-included when --columns is omitted (7 columns)
inputs.note: All 7 columns used automatically; readability advisory correctly emitted for >5 stages; advisory surfaces in Agent Response Contract; plot generated

assertions.text: parse_selected_columns returns all available columns when --columns omitted
assertions.note: Documented default behavior: empty string → all columns returned

assertions.text: Readability advisory emitted for >5 stages (7 stages detected)
assertions.note: log_warn fires in run_analysis.R when length(selected_columns) > 5

assertions.text: SKILL_EMPTY_DATA would be emitted if fewer than 2 columns available
assertions.note: validate_annotation_data() checks length(colnames(data)) < 2 and length(selected_columns) < 2

assertions.text: Agent Response Contract includes readability warning in output summary
assertions.note: Readability warnings field populated with advisory text from log_warn

assertions.text: Plot is produced (advisory is non-blocking)
assertions.note: log_warn does not halt execution; analysis continues after advisory

inputs.label: Custom output prefix, alpha, and label_size parameters
inputs.note: Custom prefix my_cohort produces my_cohort.pdf; alpha and label_size validated and applied; security validation confirmed; perfect basic score

assertions.text: --output_prefix validated for safe characters (alphanumeric, dot, underscore, hyphen only)
assertions.note: validate_output_prefix() uses grepl('^[A-Za-z0-9._-]+$', output_prefix) before analysis

assertions.text: Custom prefix my_cohort produces my_cohort.pdf in plot/ directory
assertions.note: Output filename follows {prefix}.pdf naming pattern in write_outputs()

assertions.text: --alpha 0.3 correctly accepted as valid numeric in [0,1]
assertions.note: is.finite(opt$alpha) && opt$alpha >= 0 && opt$alpha <= 1 check passes

assertions.text: --label_size 5 correctly accepted as positive numeric
assertions.note: is.finite(opt$label_size) && opt$label_size > 0 check passes

assertions.text: SKILL_INVALID_PARAMETER emitted for output_prefix containing invalid special characters
assertions.note: validate_output_prefix() stops with SKILL_INVALID_PARAMETER for characters outside allowed set

inputs.label: Six-stage Sankey with 10+ unique values per stage
inputs.note: Both readability advisories triggered correctly (>5 stages, >8 unique values); plot generated; advisory text surfaces in Agent Response Contract

assertions.text: Stage-count advisory emitted when >5 stages selected (6 stages)
assertions.note: log_warn fires: 'More than 5 stages selected (6); plot may be hard to read. Consider filtering.'

assertions.text: Unique-value advisory emitted for stage with 10 unique values
assertions.note: log_warn fires: 'Stage s3 has 10 unique values; consider aggregating for readability.'

assertions.text: Plot generated successfully despite readability boundary inputs (advisories non-blocking)
assertions.note: log_warn does not halt execution; ggalluvial handles high-cardinality inputs

assertions.text: Agent Response Contract readability warnings field populated with both advisory messages
assertions.note: Both advisory lines present in readability warnings field of agent response

assertions.text: session_info.txt written correctly despite high-complexity input
assertions.note: write_session_info() called after analysis; unaffected by stage count or unique value count

key_strengths: Agent Response Contract added — callers now receive a structured summary with stage count, sample count, output paths, and readability warnings in a single parseable block
Runtime readability advisories now enforced in run_analysis.R via log_warn for both >5-stage and >8-unique-value-per-stage thresholds, closing the documentation-to-code gap
All three reference files (algorithm.md, troubleshooting.md, cli-guide.md) are now bundled in references/, eliminating missing-file errors for agents following the When-to-Read table
Real academic citations (Brunson 2020 ggalluvial DOI) and validate_output_prefix() path-traversal guard remain strong security and credibility assets
Perfect agent-specific score (20/20): trigger precision, progressive disclosure, composability, idempotency, and escape hatches all fully implemented

recommendations.title: NA replacement count not reported in session info or advisories
recommendations.problem: When blank strings or NA values are replaced with --missing_label, no count of replacements is logged or written to session_info.txt. Users cannot audit how many samples had missing stage values.
recommendations.root_cause: normalize_stage_values() performs replacement silently; no count accumulation or log_warn for non-zero NA counts.
recommendations.fix: In normalize_stage_values(), count NA replacements per column and call log_warn(sprintf('Stage %s: %d NA/blank values replaced with %s', col, count, missing_label)) when count > 0. Optionally write per-column NA counts to session_info.txt.
